body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

## Scenario

The `user-blocking-question` primitive is `tool_calls` + AskUserQuestion (or adapter equivalent): the agent pauses **mid-turn** to ask the user a structured question via a dedicated question tool, the dashboard enters `waiting`, and stays there until the user replies — producing the user-observable arc `ready → working → waiting → working → ready` with the `waiting` episode visible (no skip-over).

This is distinct from `tool-gate-permission-prompt` (permission, not a question), `user-blocking-plan-mode-approval` (pause for plan review), and `mid-turn-message-queued` (USER sends unsolicited input; the agent does not request it).

## Reasoning — agent_supports: no

Stock pi (v0.70.0) ships **no dedicated question / elicitation tool**. The built-in tool registry is closed and explicit:

```
allToolNames = new Set(["read", "bash", "edit", "write", "grep", "find", "ls"]);
```

(`dist/core/tools/index.js`). The `dist/core/tools/` directory contains exactly those tools (plus helpers: edit-diff, truncate, path-utils, render-utils, file-mutation-queue, tool-definition-wrapper). There is no `ask`, `question`, `confirm`, `input`, or `elicit` tool.

The Step 2.5 binary guard backs this up. pi is a Node bundle; the real code lives under `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/` (the `cli.js` `which pi` resolves to is a 530-byte launcher). A recursive grep across the whole dist for `askuserquestion|ask_user|askuser|user_question|userquestion|elicit|requestuserinput|request_user_input|prompt_user|promptuser` returned **zero matches**, and a grep of the tool dir for any tool registered with a `name: "ask|question|confirm|input|elicit"` was also empty. A grep of the session writers (`agent-session.js`, `session-manager.js`) for `waiting|waitingForUser|awaiting|blockOnUser` found nothing — pi has no notion of a turn that blocks awaiting structured user input.

The agent's tools all **auto-execute** within a continuous working turn (`permission_hooks: false`, `subagents: false` in capabilities.json); there is no primitive that suspends the turn to solicit a user answer. An extension could add one, but that is not stock pi.

## Reasoning — daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

Because the agent cannot produce the `working → waiting → working` arc, there is nothing to observe and `irrlicht_observes` is `n/a` rather than `no`. Even if it could, the transport confirms there is no path: the pi parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`) maps only `session` / `model_change` / `thinking_level_change` / `branch_summary` / `custom` / `compaction` and `message` lines (roles `assistant`, `user`, `toolResult`, `bashExecution`). It emits `turn_done` only on `message.stopReason == "stop"`, `assistant` mid-turn otherwise, plus `user_message` / `tool_result`. There is **no waiting-state event kind** and no transcript signal a question tool would write that the parser recognises — so no `waiting` episode could be synthesized for pi even in principle.

## Conclusion

Verdict ratifies the column reassessment (`no` / `n/a`) and the existing coverage matrix (`no` / `n/a`). No matrix drift. The pipeline is frozen at the agent layer; what would unblock it is a future pi release shipping a dedicated AskUserQuestion-style blocking tool (re-run the keyword guard against the new binary) AND a pi-parser change to map that tool to a waiting transition.
caveats: Frozen at the agent layer: a third-party pi extension could register a question tool, but that is not stock pi and is out of scope for the matrix.
Even if pi gained such a tool, the pi parser has no waiting-state mapping today — observability would require an adapter change as well, not just an agent change.

sources.note: ParseLine handles only session/model_change/thinking_level_change/branch_summary/custom/compaction/message (assistant/user/toolResult/bashExecution); no waiting-state event kind, no question-tool mapping.
Step 2.5 guard: allToolNames = {read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls} — closed registry, no ask/question/confirm/input/elicit tool. pi v0.70.0.

Step 2.5 binary scan: grep for askuserquestion|ask_user|user_question|elicit|request_user_input|prompt_user across the whole dist returned zero matches; session writers have no waiting/awaiting/blockOnUser. Empty result cited per the guard.
permission_hooks: false, subagents: false — auto-executing tools, no mechanism to suspend a turn awaiting structured user input.
